_comment: Bonsai city-sigil bake manifest. One sigil per meditation city referenced by the current hero catalog (17 cities). Each sigil is a heraldic emblem appropriate to the city's lore region (city.ts Old Name). cityId 0 (the 'everywhere' sentinel for crypto-icon heroes) uses the existing sanctuary-meditation cairn icon and is NOT baked here.
preamble: Single heraldic medallion emblem. One isolated symbolic device centered on the canvas, no scene, no figure, no character.

style.sigil: Style: a single heraldic emblem rendered as fine antique gold (#C9A961) line work on a pure solid white (#FFFFFF) background. ABSOLUTELY no figure, no character, no person, no face, no text, no letters, no numbers — only the symbolic emblem. Medieval heraldic engraving style. Single-weight strokes, no shading, no gradient, no fills. Symmetrical about the vertical axis.

tail: Composition: perfectly centered, square 1:1, the emblem fills the central 60% of the frame on a clean uniform solid white background. Emblem only — no text, no letters, no numbers, no clutter.

sigils.subject: A heraldic fleur-de-lis with a small crown above it, Frankish royal device.
A heraldic Roman aquila — a stylized eagle with outstretched wings perched on a horizontal staff, viewed from the front.
A stylized Greek owl perched front-facing on an olive branch, with the bird's face turned away so no facial features are visible, ancient Athenian symbol.
A Germanic black-bear paw print inside a hexagonal heraldic boss, with small geometric flourishes at the hexagon vertices.
A Slavic double-headed eagle silhouetted with wings outspread, both heads turned away from the viewer so no facial features are visible.
A Byzantine star-and-crescent mark — a crescent moon embracing a stylized eight-pointed star, with a thin double-ring around them.
An Egyptian ankh symbol with a stylized sun disc above it, flanked by two outstretched falcon wings, the falcon's head replaced by the sun disc so no face is visible.
An Arabian crossed-scimitars-and-palm device — two curved scimitars crossed in saltire with a tall date palm rising between them and a small crescent moon at the top.
A Mesopotamian winged-bull lamassu silhouette in profile, with the bull's head turned away so no facial features are visible, framed by stepped ziggurat outlines on each side.
A West African adinkra-style symbol — a stylized geometric mark resembling a stylized sankofa bird turning back on itself, with the bird's head obscured by a small heraldic disc.
A Japanese mon-style family crest — a stylized circular emblem of three overlapping cherry-blossom petals arranged radially with a thin outer ring.
A Chinese imperial dragon coiled into a circle biting its own tail, with the dragon's head turned away so no facial features are visible, framed by a thin double ring.
A Chinese merchant junk ship silhouette with battened sails fully unfurled and a small pennant atop the mast, sailing across a stylized wave.
A heraldic merlion silhouette — the upper half a stylized lion with its face obscured by a deep mane shadow, the lower half a flowing fish tail, framed by a thin circular ring.
An Indian heraldic ashoka chakra — a thin circular wheel with twenty-four evenly spaced spokes radiating from the hub, with a small lotus blossom at the very center.

A Mesoamerican stylized stepped pyramid viewed from the front with a small sun disc above its apex, framed by two curving feathered-serpent silhouettes coiling up each side with their heads turned away so no facial features are visible.
A Southern African heraldic device — two crossed assegai spears behind a tall oval Nguni-style shield with horizontal banded markings, framed by stylized acacia branches on each side.
